records.ety: [OE. þrobben; of uncertain origin; cf. Russ. trepete a trembling, and E. trepidation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Throbbed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Throbbing.]

defs: To beat, or pulsate, with more than usual force or rapidity; to beat in consequence of agitation; to palpitate; -- said of the heart, pulse, etc.
quotes: My heart Throbs to know one thing.
Here may his head lie on my throbbing breast.

defs: A beat, or strong pulsation, as of the heart and arteries; a violent beating; a papitation:
quotes: The impatient throbs and longings of a soul That pants and reaches after distant good.
